data.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Slides to 2-Month Low as Inflation Drifts Down
data.description:
The 10-year Treasury yield is down by 4 bps to 4.064% on Friday, marking the lowest level since December 2025.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A lower-than-expected Consumer Price Index (CPI) reading for January contributed to the decline. Inflation rose by 2.4%, below the estimate of 2.5% and marking the lowest rate of growth since May 2025. Easing inflation strengthens the case for interest rate cuts, increasing demand for bonds and pushing yields lower.

Why Is the 10-Year Treasury Yield Down Today?

While the Fed doesn't directly control long-term yields, cooler inflation lowers expectations for future interest rates and reduces inflation risk, making Treasuries more attractive to investors.

The central bank is scheduled to meet for its next Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) on March 18. The odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the meeting are slim, with the CME FedWatch tool pricing in a 9.8% chance. However, those odds increase to 26.4% at the next meeting on April 29.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Film Pros Plan "Tooth and Nail" Fight against Warner / Netflix Deal, Warner Bros. Discovery Stock (NASDAQ:WBD) Slips
data.description:
Entertainment giant Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) may be facing a proxy fight over the deal to be bought by Netflix (NFLX), but it may have an even bigger fight on its hands from the film industry itself. Stuart Ford, a major name in the independent film circuit, has some dire pronouncements and deep concerns about the state of the film industry should this deal go through. Warner shareholders seemed concerned as well, and shares slipped fractionally in the closing minutes of Friday's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Stuart Ford is chairman of AGC Studios, and he made it clear that a Netflix/Warner deal would be a disaster. Sufficient disaster, in fact, to require the strongest possible response. Basically, a deal like that would turn entertainment professionals into the equivalent of an "Uber (UBER) driver" that would cost the entire industry talent. Ford called on professionals to "…fight tooth and nail to preserve the business culture of money flowing through the system…."

Ford further noted, "We're only just now starting to feel the effects in the industry of the flow of money having become interrupted, if not completely cut off, over the last 10 years as a result of streaming becoming such a major part of the distribution pipeline." The biggest objection seems to flow from Netflix's tendency to keep residuals and other profit participation types out of the hands of filmmakers, which would effectively reduce them to per-piece talent. If sufficient quantities of talent rebel, meanwhile, it could mean a big win for other streaming providers, and a huge potential loss for Netflix, which depends on fresh content to keep viewers.

Political Wrestlers?

Meanwhile, a recent event during All Elite Wrestling (AEW) saw Brody King removed from the schedule for a while. Some questioned whether or not it was related to anti-Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) chants before one of his matches. King has been clear in his opposition to ICE, but following the fans' chants of support, King did not appear on the following episode of AEW Dynamite, reports noted.

However, this was not deliberate, Warner noted. Warner clarified in a statement, saying, "Warner Bros. Discovery did not have any involvement in Brody King's upcoming AEW schedule. Any speculation to the contrary is categorically false. Brody is scheduled to appear during the next AEW event, which will air this Saturday on TNT and HBO Max."

Is WBD Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on WBD stock based on four Buys and six Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 171.86% rally in its share price over the past year, the average WBD price target of $28.86 per share implies 3.22% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Rigetti (RGTI) Overvalued? Top Analyst Downgrades Amid Funding Needs, Rival Gains
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Krish Sankar downgraded Rigetti Computing (RGTI) stock from Buy to Hold, citing higher risks from new fab funding needs and greater competitive headwinds. Even so, Sankar kept his forecast estimates unchanged and refrained from assigning a price target. He views RGTI as offering a balanced risk-reward profile, given its premium valuation relative to peers like IonQ Inc. (IONQ) and D-Wave Quantum (QBTS). RGTI stock plunged 8.8% after the downgrade.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sankar is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #40 out of the 12,108 analysts tracked. He has a 66% success rate and an impressive average return per rating of 41.90%.

RGTI Offers Balanced Risk-Reward

Sankar calls Rigetti a leader in superconducting gate-based quantum technology. The company targets a 1,000+ qubit QPU by 2027, aiming for 99.8% two-qubit fidelity. He stated that progress remains on track overall, though the 108-qubit Cepheus-1 chip faced a one-quarter delay.

Here are the three key reasons behind Sankar's changed view on RGTI stock:

Risk from New Fab Funding Needs

Sankar believes that RGTI may need a new fab around 2028 to push chip fidelity above 99.9% via modular chiplet designs. The recent 108-qubit QPU delay underscores quality risks at scale, with advanced semiconductor tools essential for hitting 10,000+ qubits. A new 200/300mm fab could cost over $300 million, against $525 million cash on balance sheet and $70-80 million yearly burn.

Competition from DARPA (QBI)

Sankar was surprised Rigetti missed selection for DARPA's QBI Stage B program (Quantum Benchmarking Initiative), posing greater industry competition. QBI tests quantum tech for utility-scale use, with Stage B offering $1 million+ quarterly funding versus Stage A's $0.3 million. Key benefits include gaining insights on future system needs and access to multi-year quantum funding programs.

Elevated Valuation vs. Peers

Sankar highlighted that RGTI trades at a higher valuation multiple than its quantum peers, despite lagging group averages in revenue and customer contracts. For reference, RGTI trades at a higher EV/Sales (enterprise value-to-sales) multiple of 255x, compared to IONQ's 205.7x, and QBTS' 167x. He views consensus 2027 revenue estimates as overly aggressive given limited visible opportunities ahead.

Is RGTI a Good Stock to Buy?

On TipRanks, Rigetti Computing has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on five Buys and two Hold ratings. The average Rigetti Computing price target of $38 implies 153.5% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, RGTI stock has surged more than 22%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Jumps as Novelis Plans to Come Back Online Emerge
data.description:
So we were all starting to wonder when, if ever, legacy automaker Ford's (F) major aluminum supplier Novelis would come back online fully. We were expecting it by the end of the year, but subsequent fires put a bit of a damper on that goal. But new word says that plant will be back up and running soon enough, and that was good news for Ford investors. Ford shares gained nearly 2% in Friday afternoon's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The repairs have been going on for quite some time now, but now, Novelis expects to have its Oswego plant fully operational before June is out. The repairs will set Novelis back significantly, with reports noting that the total cost of repairs is up around $255 million. But the damage to Ford has been significantly worse, as the fires have cost Ford around $2 billion in lost sales and the need to import aluminum from elsewhere, often under tariff.

Further, the loss of aluminum has weighed heavily on the F-150, which is one of Ford's major sellers. But this is likely to come to an end soon, as Ford's chief financial officer Sherry House noted, "We've got people on the ground there, we know exactly what is going on and where things stand. And we have contingency plans."

Valentine's Day Massacre

This Saturday will be a difficult one for around 1,600 workers in Kentucky, as Ford retools a battery plant that it established as part of a joint venture with SK On. It was perhaps the oddest move Ford had made in some time, reports note; while Ford laid off 1,600 workers, it plans to change the battery plant into a slightly different kind of battery plant, which will require it to hire over 2,100 workers.

This led the laid-off 1,600 workers to wonder why they simply were not retrained, instead of being let go with eligibility to be rehired later. One part of that might have something to do with the lag involved; it will take some time to make the changes. Further complicating matters is the fact that the United Auto Workers (UAW) union has a presence in that same battery plant. The UAW has not yet commented about the layoffs.

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on two Buys, 11 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 46.1% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.95 per share implies 1.17% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Tesla's January Sales Drop 17%, But Its Market Share May Have Risen
data.description:
EV maker Tesla (TSLA) saw another drop in U.S. electric-vehicle sales in January, according to state registration data. Sales are estimated to have declined by about 17% for the month. This continues a downward trend that began last year and was made worse by the expiration of the federal EV tax credit. Without that incentive in place, demand appears to have softened further at the start of the year.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nevertheless, Tesla may have actually gained market share during the month. Overall, U.S. EV sales are estimated to have fallen by more than 20% in January, which means that Tesla's decline was less severe than the broader market. In response to the weaker demand, Tesla has reduced prices on several key models in the U.S. to attract buyers. Meanwhile, some competitors have scaled back their EV projects as they shift their focus toward improving profitability.

Looking at the bigger picture, Tesla delivered approximately 1.64 million vehicles in 2025, down 8.6% from 2024 levels. This is the company's second straight year of annual declines after delivering around 1.81 million vehicles in 2023. Following years of fast growth, including 38% year-over-year increase in 2023, deliveries flattened in 2024 and then fell in 2025. This was due to slower global EV demand, rising competition, and internal model transitions.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $396.80 per share implies 5.3% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Why Is Gold Up Today, 2/13/26?
data.description:
Gold (XAUUSD) is up by over 2% on Friday and has reclaimed the $5,000 level after a cooler-than-expected inflation print led to rising rate cut odds. Gold tends to benefit when rates are lower, as the opportunity cost of holding the precious metal relative to interest-bearing assets, like Treasuries, decreases.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On an annual basis, the Consumer Price Index (CPI) fell to 2.4% in January from 2.7% in December, marking the lowest rate of growth since May 2025. January's core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, was 2.5%, the lowest level since March 2021.

Gold Rebounds as Rate Cut Hopes Grow

While a rate cut at the next Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting on March 18 is unlikely, the odds of a 25 bps reduction at the April 29 meeting climbed to 26.4% from 23.9% a day ago. The odds are even higher at the June 17 meeting, sitting at 52.3% from 48.9% yesterday.

Gold's move today "suggests the correction may have overshot, with bargain‑hunting and position‑adjustments now providing support," said ING Bank commodity strategist Ewa Manthey. The precious metal has returned 16.4% year-to-date and has completely recovered its losses over the past week.

data.title: 'Fix Your AI': Elon Musk Rips Into Anthropic after $380B Valuation
data.description:
AI startup Anthropic raised $30 billion in Series G funding, giving the company a post-money valuation of $380 billion. Despite the funding milestone, Elon Musk sharply criticized Anthropic's AI, telling the company to "Fix it." Musk, who runs the rival AI firm xAI, posted on X that Claude AI "hates Whites & Asians, especially Chinese, heterosexuals, and men," calling it "misanthropic and evil."

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Musk has previously also pointed out the irony in Anthropic's name, saying the human-focused AI company has ended up producing technology he calls "misanthropic."

Anthropic's New Funding Round

Anthropic's latest Series G funding round was led by GIC and Coatue. The round was co-led by D. E. Shaw Ventures, Dragoneer, Founders Fund, ICONIQ, and MGX.

In its official announcement, Anthropic said the funding will help boost its research, product development, and infrastructure, reinforcing its role as a leader in enterprise AI and coding. The company also noted that in less than three years since earning its first dollar in revenue, its run-rate revenue has already reached $14 billion, growing more than tenfold each year.

Musk vs. Anthropic

Anthropic is best known for its Claude large language models and competes directly with xAI's chatbot Grok. Musk has been critical of Anthropic before, with reports suggesting the startup cut off xAI's access to Claude models.

This latest confrontation also highlights rising tensions in the AI industry, where companies race to develop powerful systems while facing scrutiny over bias and safety. In this competitive landscape, success increasingly depends not only on technological breakthroughs but also on public battles among industry leaders.

Interestingly, Anthropic's rapid valuation growth reflects strong investor appetite for leading AI developers. By contrast, despite Musk's push, market data shows xAI remains a smaller player globally, with generative AI traffic still dominated by OpenAI's ChatGPT and Google's (GOOGL) Gemini.

Which Is the Best AI Stock, According to Analysts?

Most of the leading AI companies are still private, which limits direct investing options. Still, investors can gain exposure through publicly traded players tied to the AI ecosystem. data.title: Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Notches Up Despite Losses to AMD
data.description:
Chip stock Intel (INTC) has been fighting hard to get back lost market share, but some new reports say it may have quite a ways to go before it gets there. Word from Mercury Research says that one of Intel's biggest rivals, AMD (AMD) took a lot of Intel's market share, and is not eager to give it up. But despite this, Intel still managed to gain ground of its own, with a modest gain in share prices in Friday afternoon's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The recent supply strictures Intel has been operating under are proving fertile ground for AMD, reports note, with AMD now accounting for just over a third, 36%, of CPUs going into desktop systems. This time last year, AMD accounted for just over a quarter of that market at 27%. AMD's gains in laptops, meanwhile, were a bit more subdued, as AMD represented 26% of laptops against the 23.8% it held the same time the previous year.

Intel is still the clear leader in laptops, though; it accounts for 74% of the laptop PC market by itself. Given earlier reports that the laptop market is a much busier market for Intel anyway, it may have decided to concede parts of the desktop market to focus on the laptop market instead. Intel has a clear lead in the server market as well, holding 71% against AMD's 28.8%.

A Growing Need for Chip Research

Intel has been frantically trying to make a comeback from its losses over the years, but one thing that seems to go with little fanfare is chip research. Certainly, Intel has made some moves in this vein recently, as it did with the ZAM prototype. We have heard about the Nova Lake lineup and the chips to follow. But beyond that generation, where is Intel on pure research?

Increasingly, eyes in the government are looking to Intel to step in and fill in some of the gaps caused by what some call an over-reliance on domestic chip production. And Intel is certainly working in that vein; reports suggest it has "thousands" of scientists working in its Hillsboro labs. But some question if, perhaps, this is actually enough. Overseas labs are doing their own research, and in some ways, it may be ahead of even Intel.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on eight Buys, 21 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 96.95% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $48.21 per share implies 2.66% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: UBS Warns AI Could Trigger a Wave of Corporate Loan Defaults
data.description:
The stock market has already punished many software companies that appear vulnerable to artificial intelligence, but investment firm UBS (UBS) believes that the pressure may soon spread to credit markets. In a recent note, UBS head of credit strategy Matthew Mish warned that AI disruption could trigger a wave of corporate loan defaults. He said that tens of billions of dollars in debt, especially among private equity-owned software and data services firms, could default over the next year as these businesses face growing competitive pressure from AI.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Speaking to CNBC, Mish explained that markets are beginning to factor in what he called a "rapid, aggressive disruption scenario." According to Mish, investors had previously assumed that any meaningful AI disruption would not happen until 2027 or 2028. However, recent advancements have forced many to reassess that timeline. As a result, sentiment shifted from viewing AI as mostly positive for tech companies to viewing it as a winner-take-all dynamic, where leaders such as Anthropic and OpenAI could seriously threaten incumbents.

Interestingly, in UBS' base case, leveraged loan and private credit markets, which together total about $3.5 trillion, could see $75 billion to $120 billion in new defaults by late 2026 as default rates rise. At the same time, Mish outlined a more severe "tail risk" scenario in which defaults could reach roughly double those baseline estimates. In that case, he warned of a potential credit crunch, where funding becomes harder to access, and leveraged credit markets see a repricing. Still, UBS is not yet predicting that outcome, noting that much depends on how quickly companies adopt AI.

Is IGV Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) stock based on 91 Buys, 22 Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IGV price target of $136.49 per share implies 64.3% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
